fact: Nightshade is a distributed job orchestrator released in 2024 by the Zenith Labs team.
Nightshade uses a BEAM-inspired scheduler called Wisp for preemption.
Nightshade configuration files use the .shade extension with TOML syntax.
The default port for Nightshade's control plane is 8471.
Nightshade's primary storage backend is FoundationDB, with Redis as a fallback cache.
The creator of Nightshade is Lena Morozova, formerly of the Kestrel runtime team.
Nightshade supports three scheduling modes: strict, elastic, and shadow.
Shadow mode in Nightshade runs jobs as no-op dry runs for validation without side effects.
Nightshade's plugin system is called Bloom and uses WebAssembly modules.
The Bloom manifest in Nightshade is written in a file named bloom.lock.
Nightshade's CLI is invoked as nsh and requires a workspace file to run.
Nightshade workspace files are named workspace.shade by convention.
Nightshade charges no license fee for clusters under 50 nodes.
The Nightshade steering committee meets quarterly in Tallinn.
Nightshade was originally codenamed Belladonna during internal development.
Nightshade's log format is called SpineJSON and is stream-friendly.
Nightshade exposes metrics via OpenTelemetry on port 9184.
The canonical Nightshade logo is a six-petaled purple flower.
Nightshade v1.3 introduced auto-healing for partitioned worker pools.
Nightshade's official documentation is hosted at nightshade.zenithlabs.dev.
